evidence_refs.source_quote: Community Board 11 Public Safety & Transportation Committee

rationale: The observation's base_record_id 'project_116th-st-bus-pedestrian-priority' already matches the canonical registry record. Both describe the same project: bus and pedestrian priority improvements on 116th Street in Manhattan (offset bus lanes, pedestrian islands, bus queue jump signals, left turn lanes, parking-protected bike lanes). The registry card's source_ids already include the observation's source (116_st_morningside_ave_pleasant_ave_cb11_may2025). The CB11 May 2025 presentation is simply a different venue/timing for the same proposed project as the CB10 June 2025 presentation cited in the registry. Other similar registry records (project_116th-st-morningside-to-pleasant-bus-priority-review, project_116th-street-study-manhattan) are studies/reviews rather than the project itself.

evidence_refs.source_quote: 116 th Street Bus and Pedestrian Priority Project
116 th Street Bus & Pedestrian Priority Project Overview*
Project Summary*

rationale: Observation's base_record_id is corridor_116th-street, which is already the correct registry record. The registry card for corridor_116th-street already includes the source 116_st_morningside_ave_pleasant_ave_cb11_may2025 in its source_ids, and the observation label '116th Street Corridor (5th Avenue to Pleasant Avenue)' refers to the same 116th Street study area corridor in Manhattan. Since the observation already resolves to its own base_record_id (the closest match), no link to a different record is needed. Decision is new per the 'own base_record_id' rule.
